response.justification: Severe mismatch on all mandatory technical requirements. The candidate's C++ experience is limited to approximately 6 months at National Instruments (10 years ago) and a thesis project—far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on C++ development. No evidence of Windows platform development, no SQL Server/Oracle experience (only NoSQL/MongoDB), and completely lacks the required financial domain expertise (fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking). While communication skills are strong, they cannot compensate for the absence of core technical prerequisites.
strengths: Demonstrated strong communication and teaching ability through multiple talks, workshops, and mentoring roles
Senior-level software engineer with clear software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, clean architecture)
Some Java background and CI/CD pipeline experience, which are desirable skills
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and outdated—only ~6 months of recent C++ work a decade ago; entire recent career is JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js
No evidence of Windows platform development or SQL Server/Oracle expertise; career focused on Linux and NoSQL (MongoDB)
Zero domain knowledge in fixed income, capital markets, investment banking, or financial services—requirements cannot be substituted without strong C++ background
